rows.notes: Clear centered page title at top. Very legible script and no monetary columns present on this line.

rows.description: Freeman, 2 years Rent to Mich. 1849, left by deductions
rows.notes: Amounts aligned in the right-hand columns as '16 1 10½'. Pounds and shillings clear; pence shows a halfpenny symbol (loop/ob) interpreted as 0.5. Handwriting of description is slightly abbreviated but readable.
Amount reads '18 8 9½' with a clear halfpenny mark. Description uses 'Do - Do' (ditto) shorthand; name 'Heeley' legible though script could be mistaken for a similar name.

rows.description: Chancery Divs 2 yrs Taken by Rail R. at York
rows.notes: Monetary columns read clearly as '23 2 8'. Several words in the description are abbreviated and somewhat faded ('Taken by Rail R. at York' is an interpretation of the script); moderate uncertainty in exact wording of place/agent.

rows.description: Of the Dean towards purchase of the fee of New Buildings, of the City
rows.notes: Large, well-aligned amount '2100 0 0' is very clear in the pounds column. Description wording is legible though lengthy; alignment confirms columns.

rows.description: Of Mr Lightfoot 4th instalment of Loan
rows.notes: Amount '40 0 0' clearly aligned in £ s d columns. Name 'Mr Lightfoot' and '4th instalment of Loan' are legible.

rows.description: Heeley 1/2 yrs Rent of House in Queen St to Day 1850, less by deductions
rows.notes: Amount reads '15 0 4' with good alignment. Description contains fractions and abbreviations ('1/2 yrs', 'to Day 1850') that are somewhat cramped; 'Queen St' reading is probable but there is minor ambiguity in street name.
Amount '16 1 3½' is aligned; halfpenny sign visible. Description uses ditto marks; handwriting for the name is consistent with earlier 'Freeman' entry but slightly less distinct.

rows.description: Arrears of Rent for Bampton Test to Mich. 1849
rows.notes: Amounts read '40 13 6' clearly. The phrase 'Bampton Test' is the best reading of the script; minor uncertainty in the second word but amount alignment is unambiguous.

rows.description: Compensation to Donns for fire on Menton one year to Day 1850 P.D.D.
rows.notes: Amount columns read '48 10 10' clearly. The description contains several unclear names/words ('Donns', 'Menton'); interpreted conservatively from cursive letters, hence moderate confidence.

rows.description: The Bursar Incrementum Donns to Mich 1849
rows.notes: Amount '366 6 0' is well aligned and clear. 'Bursar' and 'Incrementum' readable; following words abbreviated/latinized ('Donns') — wording transcribed as seen with reasonable confidence.
Amount '1071 5 5' clear in columns. 'Do Do' indicates repetition; year '1850' legible. Slight ink variation but numeric values unambiguous.
Amount '20 0 0' clearly written and aligned. Description explicitly references '4th instalment on Loan of £200' and is quite legible.

rows.description: Dr Shortbridges Trustees to Xmas 1849 less by Prof &c & other deductions
rows.notes: Amount '211 11 8' readable though the hundreds place is slightly cramped. The trustee name and the words after 'less by' are abbreviated and somewhat faded; 'Prof &c' is an interpreted expansion of a common abbreviation seen in the script.
Amount columns read '29 2 6' clearly. The description's proper nouns are abbreviated and faint ('Donns', 'South' are best readings of the cursive).

This line shows the description 'Newington repairs — P.D.D.' but no clear amounts in the £/s/d columns (likely included in a grouped brace with adjacent lines). Ink is faint here and alignment to numeric columns is ambiguous, hence low confidence.
Amount reads clearly as '10 10 0' aligned to the £ s d columns. Description is short and legible.
Pounds column shows '30' and shillings/pence columns are blank/zero-aligned (interpreted as '30 0 0'). The word 'Dean's Fees' is legible albeit written small.
Amount appears as '5 15 2' with clear digits for pounds/shillings/pence. The description contains abbreviations ('Intt' for interest, 'P.D.D.') but is interpretable from the script.
Amount '14 9 0' is aligned and legible. 'Property Tax' wording is clear; slight ink fading but numbers are unambiguous.
Final underlined total at bottom-right reads '1076 19 0'. Numeric alignment and the ruled underline make this reading secure. No description text on this line; treated as the page total.
